L3.target: edu A+F lattice — collective dynamics emergence

purpose: Pre-register observables BEFORE lattice simulation results are inspected, to prevent post-hoc cherry-pick. Any measurement run claiming L3 emergence must satisfy ALL three observables per this frozen spec. Retroactive loosening prohibited under V8 SAFE_COMMIT (forward-only rev bump allowed with explicit justification + old_rev preserved).
risk_addressed: A simple N unit-cell parallel run is L2 x N, not L3. Emergence requires non-decomposable collective properties that a single cell cannot exhibit.

observables.name: collective_phase_transition
observables.definition: The lattice-level phase (ordered/disordered/critical) is NOT the algebraic sum or majority-vote of individual cell phases. Formally, let phi_i be the unit-cell phase indicator (0=disordered, 1=ordered) of cell i in an N-cell lattice, and Phi_L be the lattice-level phase indicator derived from the global order parameter (e.g. lattice-wide magnetization-analog, or coherent-edge fraction over the collective atlas). Emergence = Phi_L != f_decomp(phi_1, ..., phi_N) for any decomposable f (sum, mean, max, mode).
observables.measurement: For each lattice run at control parameter lambda (e.g. coupling strength or broadcast rate): (i) compute per-cell phi_i via L2 fixpoint assessment, (ii) compute Phi_L via global order parameter chi_L = |sum_i exp(i*theta_i)| / N where theta_i is cell orientation in shared concept-atlas basis, (iii) compare observed Phi_L to predicted Phi_pred = mean(phi_i). Sweep lambda across [lambda_min, lambda_max] with >= 20 grid points, >= 5 seeds each.
observables.null_hypothesis: H0: Phi_L(lambda) = mean(phi_i(lambda)) for all lambda (perfect decomposability — lattice is L2 x N).
observables.threshold: Reject H0 iff: (a) |Phi_L - mean(phi_i)| > 0.15 at some lambda*, AND (b) a sharp transition exists: |d Phi_L / d lambda| at lambda* exceeds max_i(|d phi_i / d lambda|) by >= 3x, AND (c) finite-size scaling: transition sharpness grows with N (tested at N in {16, 64, 256}).
observables.falsification: O1 FAILS if for all lambda the predicted Phi_pred matches observed Phi_L within 0.15 absolute, OR if no sharpening with N is observed (slope ratio <= 1.5x across N scales).

observables.name: non_local_correlation
observables.definition: Pairwise correlation C(i,j) between cell states decays SLOWER than any local-interaction kernel (Coulomb-analog 1/r^2 in lattice-graph distance r). Emergent long-range coherence = exists a regime where C(r) >> baseline_shuffled(r) AND C(r) exceeds the 1/r^2 envelope at r >> 1.
observables.measurement: (i) Compute C(i,j) = corr(state_i(t), state_j(t)) over T >= 200 timesteps per run, bin by graph distance r, (ii) build shuffled baseline by randomizing cell labels (preserving marginal distributions) and recomputing C_shuffled(r), (iii) compute p-value of C(r) vs C_shuffled(r) per r-bin via permutation test (>= 1000 shuffles), (iv) fit C(r) to a*r^(-alpha) + b and report alpha.
observables.null_hypothesis: H0: C(r) ~ C_shuffled(r) (no long-range order) OR alpha >= 2 (decays no slower than 1/r^2).
observables.threshold: Reject H0 iff: (a) at some r* >= sqrt(N)/2, C(r*) > C_shuffled(r*) with p < 0.05 (Bonferroni-corrected across r-bins), AND (b) fitted alpha < 1.5 with 95% CI upper bound < 2.0, AND (c) correlation length xi extracted from fit satisfies xi > lattice_diameter / 4.
observables.falsification: O2 FAILS if any of: p >= 0.05 at all r-bins, OR fitted alpha CI includes 2.0, OR xi <= lattice_diameter / 4.

observables.name: emergent_invariant
observables.definition: A conserved/bounded quantity Psi_L defined ONLY at lattice scale (no meaningful analog at single-cell scale) exists and remains stable under lattice evolution. Concretely: Phi aggregate (integrated information across cells) or Hexad closure index computed over lattice-level atlas, where the single-cell version is trivially 1 or undefined.
observables.measurement: Candidate: Psi_L = Phi_lattice defined via IIT-style partition over the cross-cell coherence graph (edges = cells sharing >= k sealed seeds). Measure Psi_L across T >= 500 timesteps. Verify: (i) Psi_L > 0.1 (non-trivial), (ii) single-cell Psi_i is undefined OR identically trivial (e.g. Phi for 1-node graph = 0 by definition), (iii) Psi_L variance over time is bounded: std(Psi_L) / mean(Psi_L) < 0.2, (iv) Psi_L is robust to 10% random cell ablation (drops by < 30%).
observables.null_hypothesis: H0: No lattice-scale quantity Psi_L exists that is both (a) non-trivial, (b) bounded in time, (c) robust to ablation, AND (d) not reducible to aggregation of single-cell quantities.
observables.threshold: Reject H0 iff ALL four sub-conditions hold: mean(Psi_L) > 0.1, std/mean < 0.2, ablation_drop < 30%, AND single_cell_Psi is provably trivial (proof by definition — e.g. 1-node IIT Phi = 0).
observables.falsification: O3 FAILS if any sub-condition violates (e.g. Psi_L collapses under small ablation, or drifts unboundedly, or reduces to sum/mean of cell-level quantities).

adjudication.L3_EMERGED: ALL three observables (O1, O2, O3) simultaneously reject their null hypotheses on the SAME lattice run (shared N, seeds, lambda sweep).
adjudication.L3_PARTIAL: 1 or 2 observables pass — report as partial, do NOT claim emergence.
adjudication.L3_FAILED: 0 observables pass — lattice is L2 x N; re-examine coupling model.
adjudication.cherry_pick_guard: Observables are frozen at rev=1. Any post-hoc threshold relaxation bumps rev and MUST preserve this rev=1 record in-file.

freeze.edu_F_application: Results from edu drill F (lattice sim) must be evaluated against rev=1 verbatim. No exceptions.
freeze.note: self-referential criteria_sha omitted (chicken-and-egg); post-commit git-blob SHA serves as the canonical freeze witness.
